project_area: Primary prevention programs for children ages 0-18, reducing juvenile delinquency, truancy, substance abuse, child abuse, and other socially destructive behaviors through strength-based activities in community-based settings
demographic_requirements: Programs must affirm and promote positive family functioning, work to impact societal forces affecting parents, children, and youth, strive for coordinated youth services, and promote racial equity and reduction of race disparities
eligibility_summary: Open to private non-profits and public entities (town government, schools, etc.) in Vermont. Programs must serve children ages 0-18 and align with primary prevention goals. Applicants must be in good standing with Vermont taxes and previous grant reports. Does not fund individuals or for-profit organizations.
